Use of f = Bq 2πm Use of T = 1/f T = 6.0 ×10 −8 s Example calculation: 푓푓= 0. 55× 1.6 ×10 −19 2π× 1.67×10 −27 = 8.39×10 Hz 푇푇= 8. 39×10 = 1.19×10 −7 s Time in one Dee 1.19×10 −10 s = 5.96×10 −8 s (1) (1) (1) *17(c) This question assesses a student’s ability to show a coherent and logically structured answer with linkages and fully-sustained reasoning. Marks are awarded for indicative content and for how the answer is structured and shows lines of reasoning. The following table shows how the marks should be awarded for indicative content. The following table shows how the marks should be awarded for structure and lines of reasoning. Indicative content IC1 (p.d. creates an) electric field between the dees IC2 Electric field/force accelerates protons IC3 Magnetic (field gives a) force at right angles to protons’ path IC4 Protons follows a circular path (whilst in dees) Or protons experience a centripetal force. IC5 p.d./polarity (of dees) switches every half cycle Or p.d./polarity switches while protons are in the dee IC6 (So) opposite side of the dee is always oppositely charged (to t he protons) so protons accelerate repeatedly IC points IC mark Max linkage mark Max final mark 6 4 2 6 5 3 2 5 4 3 1 4 3 2 1 3 2 2 0 2 1 1 0 1 0 0 0 0 Number of marks awarded for structure of answer and sustained line of reasoning Answer shows a coherent and logical structure with linkages and fully sustained lines of reasoning demonstrated throughout Answer is partially structured with some linkages and lines of reasoning Answer has no linkages between points and is unstructured Total for question 17 Number Answer Mark

The magnets/poles are moved relative to the coil/reader This causes a change in flux linkage with the coil Or This causes the coil to cut lines of flux An e.m.f. is induced across the coil The (north/south) pole affects the direction of the e.m.f. Or the sensor will register positive and negative p.d. as the polarity of the magnetic field changes. (1) (1) (1) (1)

The rubber strip deforms (when the cars collide) Increasing the time taken for the change in momentum of the car Or increasing the time for the collision So since F∆t is equal to change in momentum, the force is reduced (1) (1) (1)
